concerns.text: FR-003 mandates validating the cosine proxy via LLM consensus on a 'random [deferred] sample of flagged calls'. T013 flags calls, but T014 ('Implement LLM consensus validation...') lacks a task to explicitly filter the logged calls to the 'flagged' set before sampling. Without a task to extract the flagged subset, T014 risks sampling from the entire call log, violating the FR-003 requirement to validate the proxy specifically on high-similarity pairs.

US-1 Acceptance Scenario 1 requires comparing NDCG@10 against a 'full-baseline run on the unique subset'. T015 implements the baseline on the 'full candidate list' (redundant), and T016 calculates NDCG. No task exists to generate the 'unique subset' of the candidate list or to execute the baseline ranker against this unique subset to establish the reference NDCG required for the '15% lower' hypothesis test.

Constitution Principle III (Data Hygiene) requires datasets to be checksummed and recorded in the state file. T005 implements BEIR data fetching but contains no task to compute checksums of the raw fetched files or update the project state file, breaking the reproducibility and data hygiene constraints.

FR-002 mandates creating 'at least 20 clusters of 3–5 near-duplicate items'. T012 implements the injection logic but lacks a verification step or task to assert that the resulting dataset actually meets this specific cluster count and size constraint before proceeding to ranking, risking silent failure of the FR.

FR-006 requires the system to 'enforce' a hard runtime limit of 6h and 7GB memory. T004 only configures these values in `config.py`. No task exists to implement the actual enforcement mechanism (e.g., watchdog, signal handler, or resource-limited subprocess) that terminates the pipeline if limits are exceeded, leaving the requirement unverified at runtime.

FR-003 requires the LLM consensus sample size to be a 'random [deferred] sample' (implying a fraction of the budget or flagged set). No task exists to calculate this sample size dynamically based on the total calls made in the current run before executing T014, risking under-sampling (invalid stats) or over-sampling (budget exhaustion).
